message:
Remove colored crate dependency, migrate to rich_rust

- Remove colored = "3.0" from Cargo.toml
- Update print_human_quick_start() to use rich_rust Console/Style/Text
- Update cmd_snapshots() list output to use rich_rust
- Update cmd_snapshot_delete() confirmation to use rich_rust
- Update cmd_snapshot_show() to use rich_rust for styled labels
- Remove colored::control::set_override() call (now handled by rich_rust)
- Remove unused IsTerminal import

This completes bead bd-2rh7: Remove colored crate dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2qo","title":"Design snapshot storage schema","description":"## Purpose
Design comprehensive storage schema for snapshots, combining SQLite metadata with file-based image caching.

## Directory Structure
```
~/.local/share/sd/
├── snapshots/
│   ├── snapshots.db           # SQLite database
│   └── images/                # Content-addressable image cache
│       ├── aa/
│       │   └── aabbcc...123.webp
│       ├── bb/
│       │   └── bbccdd...456.webp
│       └── ...
```

## SQLite Schema
```sql
-- snapshots.db

-- Snapshot metadata
CREATE TABLE snapshots (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    description TEXT,
    device_model TEXT NOT NULL,      -- "StreamDeckXL", "StreamDeckMK2", etc.
    device_serial TEXT,              -- Optional: bind to specific device
    key_count INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- Device key count at snapshot time
    key_width INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- Image dimensions expected
    key_height INTEGER NOT NULL,
    brightness INTEGER,              -- 0-100 or NULL if not captured
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,        -- ISO 8601 timestamp
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,        -- ISO 8601 timestamp
    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1        -- Schema version for future migration
);

-- Per-key state within snapshot
CREATE TABLE snapshot_keys (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    snapshot_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES snapshots(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    key_index INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- 0-based key number
    state_type TEXT NOT NULL,        -- 'image', 'color', 'clear'
    
    -- For 'image' type
    source_path TEXT,                -- Original source path (for reference)
    image_hash TEXT,                 -- SHA256 of processed image content
    
    -- For 'color' type  
    color_hex TEXT,                  -- "#RRGGBB" format
    
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    
    UNIQUE(snapshot_id, key_index)
);

-- Image cache metadata
CREATE TABLE images (
    hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,           -- SHA256 hash (content-addressable)
    original_path TEXT,              -- First source path seen
    width INTEGER NOT NULL,
    height INTEGER NOT NULL,
    format TEXT NOT NULL,            -- "webp", "png", etc.
    size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    last_accessed_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    access_count INTEGER DEFAULT 1
);

-- Indexes for performance
CREATE INDEX idx_snapshot_keys_snapshot ON snapshot_keys(snapshot_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_snapshot_keys_hash ON snapshot_keys(image_hash);
CREATE INDEX idx_images_accessed ON images(last_accessed_at);
```

## File Storage
Images stored using content-addressable pattern:
```rust
/// Generate storage path for image hash
fn image_path(hash: &str) -> PathBuf {
    // First 2 chars as subdirectory for distribution
    let dir = &hash[0..2];
    PathBuf::from(format!("images/{}/{}.webp", dir, hash))
}
```

## Rust Structs
```rust
// src/snapshot/schema.rs
use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Snapshot {
    pub id: i64,
    pub name: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub device_model: String,
    pub device_serial: Option<String>,
    pub key_count: u8,
    pub key_width: u32,
    pub key_height: u32,
    pub brightness: Option<u8>,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub keys: Vec<SnapshotKey>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct SnapshotKey {
    pub key_index: u8,
    pub state: KeyState,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum KeyState {
    Image {
        source_path: Option<String>,
        image_hash: String,
    },
    Color {
        hex: String,
    },
    Clear,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct CachedImage {
    pub hash: String,
    pub original_path: Option<String>,
    pub width: u32,
    pub height: u32,
    pub format: String,
    pub size_bytes: u64,
}
```

## Database Access Layer
```rust
// src/snapshot/db.rs
use rusqlite::{Connection, params};
use tracing::{debug, info, instrument};

pub struct SnapshotDb {
    conn: Connection,
}

impl SnapshotDb {
    #[instrument(skip_all)]
    pub fn open() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_local_dir()
            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("No local data directory"))?
            .join("sd/snapshots/snapshots.db");
        
        std::fs::create_dir_all(path.parent().unwrap())?;
        
        let conn = Connection::open(&path)?;
        let db = Self { conn };
        db.migrate()?;
        Ok(db)
    }
    
    fn migrate(&self) -> Result<()> {
        self.conn.execute_batch(include_str!("schema.sql"))?;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    #[instrument(skip(self))]
    pub fn save_snapshot(&self, snapshot: &Snapshot) -> Result<i64> {
        // Insert or update snapshot...
    }
    
    #[instrument(skip(self))]
    pub fn load_snapshot(&self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<Snapshot>> {
        // Load snapshot with all keys...
    }
    
    #[instrument(skip(self))]
    pub fn list_snapshots(&self) -> Result<Vec<SnapshotSummary>> {
        // Return list of snapshots without full key data...
    }
    
    #[instrument(skip(self))]
    pub fn delete_snapshot(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {
        // Delete snapshot and orphaned images...
    }
}
```

## Robot Mode Output
```json
{
  "command": "save",
  "snapshot": {
    "name": "work-layout",
    "device_model": "StreamDeckXL",
    "key_count": 32,
    "brightness": 80,
    "keys_saved": 24,
    "images_cached": 18,
    "cache_size_bytes": 156789
  }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual key save/load operations
- DEBUG: SQL queries, file I/O operations
- INFO: Snapshot save/load/delete with summary
- WARN: Missing source images (use cached)
- ERROR: Database errors, I/O failures

## Success Criteria
- [ ] SQLite schema handles all key types
- [ ] Content-addressable image storage works
- [ ] Snapshots can be saved and restored correctly
- [ ] Device compatibility checked on restore
- [ ] Orphaned images cleaned up on delete
- [ ] Robot mode provides structured output","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:04.945524164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:18:19.788994646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:18:19.788937979Z","close_reason":"Implemented snapshot storage schema: SQLite schema in src/snapshot/db.rs, Rust structs (Snapshot, SnapshotKey, KeyState, CachedImage, SnapshotSummary) in src/snapshot/schema.rs, database access layer with CRUD operations, content-addressable image caching, and comprehensive tests (12 new tests passing).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qo","depends_on_id":"bd-34i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:04.973777821Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2re","title":"Write tests for snapshot system","description":"## Test Coverage for Device State Snapshots

### Unit Tests (require MockDevice - bd-180)

1. **Snapshot Serialization Tests**
   - Snapshot serializes to valid JSON
   - Snapshot deserializes correctly
   - Handles all key states: image, color, cleared
   - Preserves brightness value
   - Includes device metadata (model, serial)

2. **State Tracking Tests**
   - set-key records image path
   - fill-key records color value
   - clear-key records cleared state
   - brightness changes recorded
   - Multiple operations on same key use latest

3. **Snapshot Storage Tests**
   - Saves to ~/.local/share/sd/snapshots/
   - Creates directory if not exists
   - Uses SQLite for metadata index
   - Stores images in content-addressable format
   - Handles disk full gracefully

4. **Image Resolution Tests**
   - Original path available and valid → use original
   - Original path missing → fallback to cached
   - Both missing → clear error with recovery hint
   - Relative paths resolved correctly
   - Home directory ~ expanded correctly

### Integration Tests (require MockDevice)

1. **Save-Restore Cycle**
   - Apply configuration
   - Save snapshot
   - Clear device
   - Restore snapshot
   - Verify device state matches original

2. **Multiple Snapshots**
   - Save "work" snapshot
   - Save "gaming" snapshot
   - Restore "work" - verify correct
   - Restore "gaming" - verify correct
   - List shows both with metadata

3. **Snapshot Operations**
   - List shows all snapshots with dates
   - Show displays snapshot contents
   - Delete removes snapshot and cached images
   - Overwrite existing with same name (with confirmation)

4. **Robot Mode Output**
   - Save returns snapshot ID and path
   - Restore returns per-key application status
   - List returns JSON array with metadata
   - Errors include snapshot name and reason

### E2E Tests (require E2E harness - bd-1xy)

1. **CLI Workflow**
   - `sd brightness 80 && sd set-key 0 icon.png`
   - `sd save my-layout`
   - `sd clear-all`
   - `sd restore my-layout`
   - Verify keys restored correctly

2. **Snapshot Management**
   - `sd snapshots` lists all snapshots
   - `sd snapshot show my-layout` displays contents
   - `sd snapshot delete my-layout` removes it
   - `sd snapshot delete nonexistent` returns error

3. **Edge Cases**
   - Save with empty device state
   - Restore when source images deleted
   - Restore to different device model (should warn)
   - Unicode characters in snapshot name

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Each key being saved/restored
- DEBUG: Image resolution decisions, cache hits/misses
- INFO: Snapshot save/restore complete with key count
- WARN: Fallback to cached image, missing originals
- ERROR: Save/restore failures with context

### Test Data
- tests/fixtures/snapshots/work.json - sample snapshot file
- tests/fixtures/snapshots/gaming.json - another sample
- tests/fixtures/snapshots/corrupted.json - invalid snapshot","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:05.979311207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:42.806959662Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2re","depends_on_id":"bd-180","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:38:38.763082225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2re","depends_on_id":"bd-1xy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:42.806921039Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2re","depends_on_id":"bd-34i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:05.988259595Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2rh7","title":"Remove colored crate dependency","description":"# Remove colored crate dependency

## Purpose
After the rich_rust integration is complete, remove the colored crate dependency since all styling is now handled by rich_rust. This reduces dependencies and ensures we're not accidentally using the old styling system.

## Background
The colored crate was used for basic terminal coloring before rich_rust integration. With rich_rust now handling all styled output, colored is no longer needed.

## Pre-conditions
Before removing colored:
- [ ] All output flows through Output trait
- [ ] No code imports or uses colored
- [ ] Robot mode tests pass (unchanged JSON)
- [ ] Human mode tests pass (styled output works)

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Search for colored Usage
```bash
rg "use colored" src/
rg "colored::" src/
rg ".red()" src/   # Method-style coloring
rg ".green()" src/
rg ".blue()" src/
```

### Step 2: Verify No Usage
All results should be empty or in comments/documentation.

### Step 3: Remove from Cargo.toml
```toml
# Before
[dependencies]
colored = "3.0"

# After
# (line removed)
```

### Step 4: Verify Compilation
```bash
cargo check --all-targets
cargo build
cargo test
```

### Step 5: Verify No colored in Lock File
```bash
cargo tree | grep colored
# Should show no results
```

## Potential Issues

### Transitive Dependency
If another crate depends on colored, it may still appear in Cargo.lock. That's fine - we just don't want direct dependency.

### Test Code
Ensure test utilities don't use colored either.

### Build Scripts
Check build.rs doesn't use colored (it shouldn't).

## Verification
```bash
# Ensure no compile errors
cargo check --all-targets

# Ensure all tests pass
cargo test

# Verify binary works
cargo run -- list
cargo run -- list --robot
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] colored removed from Cargo.toml [dependencies]
- [ ] No code imports colored
- [ ] cargo check passes
- [ ] cargo test passes
- [ ] CLI works correctly in both modes","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:40.401060023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:40.445503361Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","dependencies","phase-3","rich-rust"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rh7","depends_on_id":"bd-1rhq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:40.440219512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rh7","depends_on_id":"bd-2ui5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:40.445470679Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
